Question: A point has one dimension.
A line has one dimension.
A plane has two dimensions.
A line segment has two endpoints.
A ray has two endpoints.
Two lines that meet at a point are called intersecting lines.
Two lines that never meet are called parallel lines.
A polygon is a closed figure formed by line segments.
A triangle has four sides.
A quadrilateral has four sides.
A pentagon has five sides.
A hexagon has six sides.
A heptagon has seven sides.
An octagon has eight sides.
A circle is a closed curved figure.
The distance around a circle is called radius.
Half of the diameter of a circle is called radius.
A line touching a circle at only one point is called tangent.
The corner point of a polygon is called vertex.
The line joining two vertices of a polygon is called diagonal.
An angle of 90 degrees is called a right angle.
An angle less than 90 degrees is called an acute angle.
An angle greater than 90 degrees is called an obtuse angle.
A straight angle measures 180 degrees.
A full rotation measures 360 degrees.
Two rays with a common endpoint form an angle.
A triangle with all sides equal is called equilateral.
A triangle with two sides equal is called scalene.
A triangle with no sides equal is called scalene.
A triangle with one right angle is called right-angled.
A triangle with all angles less than 90 degrees is called acute.
A triangle with one angle greater than 90 degrees is called obtuse.
A quadrilateral with four right angles is called rectangle.
A quadrilateral with four right angles and all sides equal is called square.
The space enclosed by a polygon is called perimeter.
The distance around a polygon is called perimeter.
The number of sides in a hexagon is seven.
The number of sides in a heptagon is seven.
The number of sides in an octagon is eight.
A polygon with unequal sides and angles is called regular.
A circle has vertices.
The diameter of a circle is twice its radius.
A polygon with three sides is called triangle.
A polygon with four sides is called pentagon.
A polygon with five sides is called pentagon.
A polygon with six sides is called hexagon.
The distance from centre to circumference is called diameter.
A line joining any two non-adjacent vertices of a polygon is called diagonal.
All angles in a triangle add up to 180 degrees.
All angles in a quadrilateral add up to 360 degrees.
